speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Dost thou believe, Orlando, that the boy

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Can do all this that he hath promisèd?

speaker: ORLANDO
text: I sometimes do believe and sometimes do not,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: As those that fear they hope, and know they fear.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Patience once more whiles our compact is urged.

speaker: ROSALIND
text:
To
                 
                Duke
                .
                 
              You say, if I bring in your Rosalind,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You will bestow her on Orlando here?

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That would I, had I kingdoms to give with her.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: And you say you will have her when I bring her?

speaker: ORLANDO
text: That would I, were I of all kingdoms king.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You say you’ll marry me if I be willing?

speaker: PHOEBE
text: That will I, should I die the hour after.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: But if you do refuse to marry me,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You’ll give yourself to this most faithful shepherd?

speaker: PHOEBE
text: So is the bargain.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You say that you’ll have Phoebe if she will?

speaker: SILVIUS
text: Though to have her and death were both one thing.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I have promised to make all this matter even.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Keep you your word, O duke, to give your daughter,—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: You yours, Orlando, to receive his daughter.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Keep you your word, Phoebe, that you’ll marry me,

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Or else, refusing me, to wed this shepherd.—

speaker: ROSALIND
text: Keep your word, Silvius, that you’ll marry her

speaker: ROSALIND
text: If she refuse me. And from hence I go

speaker: ROSALIND
text: To make these doubts all even.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: I do remember in this shepherd boy

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Some lively touches of my daughter’s favor.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: My lord, the first time that I ever saw him

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Methought he was a brother to your daughter.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: But, my good lord, this boy is forest-born

speaker: ORLANDO
text: And hath been tutored in the rudiments

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Of many desperate studies by his uncle,

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Whom he reports to be a great magician

speaker: ORLANDO
text: Obscurèd in the circle of this forest.

speaker: JAQUES
text: There is sure another flood toward, and these

speaker: JAQUES
text: couples are coming to the ark. Here comes a pair of

speaker: JAQUES
text: very strange beasts, which in all tongues are called

speaker: JAQUES
text: fools.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Salutation and greeting to you all.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Good my lord, bid him welcome.

speaker: JAQUES
text: This is the motley-minded gentleman that I have so

speaker: JAQUES
text: often met in the forest. He hath been a courtier, he

speaker: JAQUES
text: swears.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: If any man doubt that, let him put me to

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: my purgation. I have trod a measure. I have flattered

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: a lady. I have been politic with my friend,

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: smooth with mine enemy. I have undone three

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: tailors. I have had four quarrels, and like to have

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: fought one.

speaker: JAQUES
text: And how was that ta’en up?

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Faith, we met and found the quarrel was

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: upon the seventh cause.

speaker: JAQUES
text: How “seventh cause”?—Good my lord, like

speaker: JAQUES
text: this fellow.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: I like him very well.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: God ’ild you, sir. I desire you of the like. I

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: press in here, sir, amongst the rest of the country

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: copulatives, to swear and to forswear, according as

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: marriage binds and blood breaks. A poor virgin, sir,

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: an ill-favored thing, sir, but mine own. A poor

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: humor of mine, sir, to take that that no man else

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: will. Rich honesty dwells like a miser, sir, in a poor

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: house, as your pearl in your foul oyster.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: By my faith, he is very swift and

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: sententious.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: According to the fool’s bolt, sir, and such

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: dulcet diseases.

speaker: JAQUES
text: But for the seventh cause. How did you find the

speaker: JAQUES
text: quarrel on the seventh cause?

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Upon a lie seven times removed.—Bear

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: your body more seeming, Audrey.—As thus, sir: I

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: did dislike the cut of a certain courtier’s beard. He

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: sent me word if I said his beard was not cut well, he

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: was in the mind it was. This is called “the retort

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: courteous.” If I sent him word again it was not well

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: cut, he would send me word he cut it to please

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: himself. This is called “the quip modest.” If again it

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: was not well cut, he disabled my judgment. This is

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: called “the reply churlish.” If again it was not well

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: cut, he would answer I spake not true. This is called

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: “the reproof valiant.” If again it was not well cut, he

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: would say I lie. This is called “the countercheck

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: quarrelsome,” and so to “the lie circumstantial,”

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: and “the lie direct.”

speaker: JAQUES
text: And how oft did you say his beard was not well

speaker: JAQUES
text: cut?

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: I durst go no further than the lie circumstantial,

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: nor he durst not give me the lie direct, and

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: so we measured swords and parted.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Can you nominate in order now the degrees of

speaker: JAQUES
text: the lie?

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: O sir, we quarrel in print, by the book, as

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: you have books for good manners. I will name you

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: the degrees: the first, “the retort courteous”; the

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: second, “the quip modest”; the third, “the reply

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: churlish”; the fourth, “the reproof valiant”; the

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: fifth, “the countercheck quarrelsome”; the sixth,

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: “the lie with circumstance”; the seventh, “the lie

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: direct.” All these you may avoid but the lie direct,

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: and you may avoid that too with an “if.” I knew

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: when seven justices could not take up a quarrel, but

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: when the parties were met themselves, one of them

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: thought but of an “if,” as: “If you said so, then I said

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: so.” And they shook hands and swore brothers.

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: Your “if” is the only peacemaker: much virtue in

speaker: TOUCHSTONE
text: “if.”

speaker: JAQUES
text: Is not this a rare fellow, my lord?

speaker: JAQUES
text: He’s as good at anything and yet a fool.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: He uses his folly like a stalking-horse,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: and under the presentation of that he shoots his wit.

speaker: HYMEN
text: Then is there mirth in heaven

speaker: HYMEN
text: When earthly things made even

speaker: HYMEN
text: Atone together.

speaker: HYMEN
text: Good duke, receive thy daughter.

speaker: HYMEN
text: Hymen from heaven brought her,

speaker: HYMEN
text: Yea, brought her hither,

speaker: HYMEN
text: That thou mightst join her hand with his,

speaker: HYMEN
text: Whose heart within his bosom is.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: To you I give myself, for I am yours.

speaker: ROSALIND
text:
To
                 
                Orlando
                .
                 
              To you I give myself, for I am yours.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: If there be truth in sight, you are my daughter.

speaker: ORLANDO
text: If there be truth in sight, you are my Rosalind.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: If sight and shape be true,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Why then, my love adieu.

speaker: ROSALIND
text: I’ll have no father, if you be not he.

speaker: ROSALIND
text:
To
                 
                Orlando
                .
                 
              I’ll have no husband, if you be not he,

speaker: ROSALIND
text:
To
                 
                Phoebe
                .
                 
              Nor ne’er wed woman, if you be not she.

speaker: HYMEN
text: Peace, ho! I bar confusion.

speaker: HYMEN
text: ’Tis I must make conclusion

speaker: HYMEN
text: Of these most strange events.

speaker: HYMEN
text: Here’s eight that must take hands

speaker: HYMEN
text: To join in Hymen’s bands,

speaker: HYMEN
text: If truth holds true contents.

speaker: HYMEN
text: You and you no cross shall part.

speaker: HYMEN
text: You and you are heart in heart.

speaker: HYMEN
text: You to his love must accord

speaker: HYMEN
text: Or have a woman to your lord.

speaker: HYMEN
text: You and you are sure together

speaker: HYMEN
text: As the winter to foul weather.

speaker: HYMEN
text: Whiles a wedlock hymn we sing,

speaker: HYMEN
text: Feed yourselves with questioning,

speaker: HYMEN
text: That reason wonder may diminish

speaker: HYMEN
text: How thus we met, and these things finish.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Wedding is great Juno’s crown,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: O blessèd bond of board and bed.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: ’Tis Hymen peoples every town.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: High wedlock then be honorèd.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Honor, high honor, and renown

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: To Hymen, god of every town.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: O my dear niece, welcome thou art to me,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Even daughter, welcome in no less degree.

speaker: PHOEBE
text: I will not eat my word. Now thou art mine,

speaker: PHOEBE
text: Thy faith my fancy to thee doth combine.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Let me have audience for a word or two.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: I am the second son of old Sir Rowland,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: That bring these tidings to this fair assembly.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Duke Frederick, hearing how that every day

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Men of great worth resorted to this forest,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Addressed a mighty power, which were on foot

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: In his own conduct, purposely to take

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: His brother here and put him to the sword;

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: And to the skirts of this wild wood he came,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Where, meeting with an old religious man,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: After some question with him, was converted

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: Both from his enterprise and from the world,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: His crown bequeathing to his banished brother,

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: And all their lands restored to them again

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: That were with him exiled. This to be true

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: I do engage my life.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Welcome, young man.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Thou offer’st fairly to thy brothers’ wedding:

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: To one his lands withheld, and to the other

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: A land itself at large, a potent dukedom.—

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: First, in this forest let us do those ends

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That here were well begun and well begot,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And, after, every of this happy number

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: That have endured shrewd days and nights with us

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Shall share the good of our returnèd fortune

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: According to the measure of their states.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Meantime, forget this new-fall’n dignity,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: And fall into our rustic revelry.—

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Play, music.—And you brides and bridegrooms all,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: With measure heaped in joy to th’ measures fall.

speaker: JAQUES
text: Sir, by your patience: if I heard you rightly,

speaker: JAQUES
text: The Duke hath put on a religious life

speaker: JAQUES
text: And thrown into neglect the pompous court.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              BROTHER
text: He hath.

speaker: JAQUES
text: To him will I. Out of these convertites

speaker: JAQUES
text: There is much matter to be heard and learned.

speaker: JAQUES
text:
To
                 
                Duke
                .
                 
              You to your former honor I bequeath;

speaker: JAQUES
text: Your patience and your virtue well deserves it.

speaker: JAQUES
text:
To
                 
                Orlando
                .
                 
              You to a love that your true faith doth merit.

speaker: JAQUES
text:
To
                 
                Oliver
                .
                 
              You to your land, and love, and great allies.

speaker: JAQUES
text:
To
                 
                Silvius
                .
                 
              You to a long and well-deservèd bed.

speaker: JAQUES
text:
To
                 
                Touchstone
                .
                 
              And you to wrangling, for thy loving voyage

speaker: JAQUES
text: Is but for two months victualled.—So to your pleasures.

speaker: JAQUES
text: I am for other than for dancing measures.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Stay, Jaques, stay.

speaker: JAQUES
text: To see no pastime, I. What you would have

speaker: JAQUES
text: I’ll stay to know at your abandoned cave.

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: Proceed, proceed. We’ll begin these rites,

speaker:
DUKE
               
              SENIOR
text: As we do trust they’ll end, in true delights.
